jokes.text: and eyes that show you that he's a little boy after all. You just happened to mention him in passing?
jokes.setup: Elaine gives overly romantic description of Fred Collins
jokes.punchline: Alex's sarcastic response pointing out she's clearly obsessed
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that punctures Elaine's romantic rambling with dry observation

jokes.text: And if that doesn't work, I'll try something subtle like the Dance of the Seven Veils.
jokes.setup: Elaine talking about trying to impress Fred at dinner
jokes.punchline: Calling the extremely provocative Dance of the Seven Veils 'subtle'
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of irony - 'subtle' is the exact opposite of what the Dance of Seven Veils is. Great character moment showing Elaine's romantic desperation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you're right. It's not special enough.
jokes.setup: Character asks if she can tell the difference about her $25 hairdo
jokes.punchline: Elaine's panic when she realizes her expensive hairdo looks the same
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven humor about vanity and self-doubt

jokes.text: That's that place with the great nude beaches, right? Yeah, but they got spiders this big.
jokes.setup: Elaine mentions Vincenzo Seneca hair stylist
jokes.punchline: Jim confuses it with a location and Tony plays along with spider details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim Ignatowski moment - completely wrong but delivered with confidence. Tony's commitment to the bit makes it better.

jokes.text: Cowlick? I'll try anything.
jokes.setup: Jim mentions needing help with 'one problem area' in his hair
jokes.punchline: Tony's assumption it's a cowlick, Jim's desperate agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Tony trying to be helpful, Jim's pathetic eagerness

jokes.text: Uh, is it a volleyball?
jokes.setup: Louie asking what's under the cover with great fanfare
jokes.punchline: Tony's completely wrong guess
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Close.
jokes.setup: Tony guesses volleyball for what's under the cover
jokes.punchline: Louie's sarcastic 'close' when it's actually Pac-Man
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie delivery - sarcastic and condescending in one word

jokes.text: Iggy! You're a somewhat compulsive personality. Step forward.
jokes.setup: Louie choosing who gets first Pac-Man game
jokes.punchline: Selecting Jim specifically because he's compulsive - perfect for an addictive game
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Louie knows exactly what will happen to Jim with an addictive game. Sets up the episode's B-plot perfectly.

jokes.text: Is it over? Yeah. That was the greatest thrill of my life!
jokes.setup: Jim plays his first game of Pac-Man (presumably dies quickly)
jokes.punchline: Despite lasting only seconds, he calls it the greatest thrill of his life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim character moment - his innocent enthusiasm makes even complete failure feel like victory. Great setup for his addiction arc.

jokes.text: Elaine's internal debate about the hair appointment
jokes.setup: Elaine got the appointment she wanted
jokes.punchline: Her rapid-fire internal argument between practical and extravagant impulses
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Elaine's internal conflict between practical and romantic impulses

jokes.text: Creative people.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Vincenzo sometimes cancels appointments
jokes.punchline: Dismissive excuse delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of pretentious salon attitude - excusing bad behavior as artistic temperament

jokes.text: No. No, it's very reasonable.
jokes.setup: Learning the haircut costs $225
jokes.punchline: Elaine's shocked pause then obviously false claim it's reasonable
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - the pause sells her shock, then the obvious lie

jokes.text: Bring me a kimono. Oh, oh, I would, but I think there'll be security guards. Then bring me a security guard.
jokes.setup: Client asking how to thank Vincenzo for her hair
jokes.punchline: His increasingly outrageous requests escalating from kimono to security guard
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation establishing Vincenzo's outrageous ego and sense of entitlement

jokes.text: just as Michelangelo discovered the beauty of David in a slab of marble, I shall find in that head of hair your perfect hairstyle.
jokes.setup: Vincenzo about to cut Elaine's hair
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Michelangelo in the most pretentious way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - Vincenzo's massive ego and pretentious artistic delusions. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: Well, stick with me, and you'll become a passenger.
jokes.setup: Learning Elaine is a cab driver
jokes.punchline: Pun about going from driver to passenger as metaphor for success
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Vincenzo's character - condescending but witty

jokes.text: We'll stick with I Love Lucy.
jokes.setup: Elaine saying she's comfortable with her hair color
jokes.punchline: Vincenzo's catty comparison to Lucy's famous red hair
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment - Vincenzo's casual cruelty disguised as wit

jokes.text: Rebecca, we're gonna need some green out here. Just kidding. Just kidding!
jokes.setup: Vincenzo working on Elaine's hair
jokes.punchline: Fake request for green hair dye to scare Elaine
jokes.explanation: Shows Vincenzo's cruel streak - psychological torture disguised as playfulness

jokes.text: Too fabulous for you?
jokes.setup: Revealing Elaine's disastrous haircut
jokes.punchline: Vincenzo's condescending response to her obvious horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect villain line - blaming the victim for not appreciating his 'art'

jokes.text: Well, I guess I just can't do cabdrivers.
jokes.setup: Elaine leaving upset after the salon staff fake-praised her hair
jokes.punchline: Vincenzo's classist dismissal as she leaves crying
jokes.explanation: Perfectly despicable line that crystallizes Vincenzo's character and sets up the climax. The casual cruelty is both funny and infuriating.

jokes.text: No. I had to go out a couple of times and beg for quarters.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's been at Pac-Man all day
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's so addicted he went begging for money to continue
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Jim's addiction arc. The casual way he admits to begging is both pathetic and hilarious.

jokes.text: Yum, yum, yum, yum. Yum, yum, yum, yum.
jokes.setup: Louie handing out paychecks
jokes.punchline: Making eating sounds while enjoying Jim's financial suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - sadistic enjoyment of others' misfortune expressed through childish sounds

jokes.text: Why? So they can serve hors d'oeuvres on my head?
jokes.setup: Tony suggesting she go to the Met event anyway
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about her hair being so bad it could be used as a serving tray
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with vivid visual imagery. Shows Elaine maintaining her wit even in distress.

jokes.text: It might be worth a shot.
jokes.setup: Elaine saying if she does anything to her hair it'll fall out in clumps
jokes.punchline: Tony's matter-of-fact suggestion that baldness might be an improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed deadpan delivery implying her hair is so bad that baldness would be an upgrade. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: for making me look like spoiled coleslaw
jokes.setup: Elaine's rant about paying Vincenzo
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disgusting comparison of her hair to spoiled coleslaw
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and specific visual metaphor that's both disgusting and funny. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: They always appreciate it when you notice the little things.
jokes.setup: Jim noticing Elaine's obviously disastrous haircut
jokes.punchline: Calling the dramatic hair disaster a 'little thing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim obliviousness - treating obvious disaster as subtle detail worthy of relationship advice

jokes.text: I haven't seen a mess like that since somebody stuck a firecracker up my Uncle Emilio's parrot.
jokes.setup: Louie seeing Elaine's hair for the first time
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the aftermath of animal cruelty with a firecracker
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie line - specific, dark, absurd family history used as comparison. Perfect character voice and very memorable.

jokes.text: What insults? I loved that bird.
jokes.setup: Alex saying Louie shouldn't insult Elaine
jokes.punchline: Louie's genuine defense that he wasn't insulting her, he actually loved the exploded parrot
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that makes Louie's character even more disturbing - he genuinely thinks fondly of the exploded bird

jokes.text: I look forward to seeing you every day, like some palookas look forward to a sunrise.
jokes.setup: Louie explaining why Elaine's hair disaster affects him
jokes.punchline: Comparing his appreciation of Elaine's beauty to how simple people appreciate sunrises
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Louie moment - reveals unexpected romantic side while still being condescending ('palookas'). Great character depth.

jokes.text: If a man assaulted her body, he'd go to jail. If a man desecrated a work of art... In the clink.
jokes.setup: Louie building case that Vincenzo should be punished
jokes.punchline: Comparing Elaine's hair disaster to assault and art desecration
jokes.explanation: Good escalating legal argument with Louie's twisted logic comparing bad haircut to serious crimes

jokes.text: Reiger, you've seen his fights. There's not a court in the land that would buy that one.
jokes.setup: Tony being called a professional boxer who can't just fight people
jokes.punchline: Louie pointing out Tony's terrible boxing record makes him harmless
jokes.explanation: Perfect character slam on Tony's boxing incompetence while making legal point

jokes.text: Even you can take a hairdresser. Was he big?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tony fighting Vincenzo
jokes.punchline: Louie's confidence Tony can beat a hairdresser, Tony's practical question about size
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Louie's insult disguised as encouragement, Tony's practical concerns

jokes.text: When I was leaving there crying, he said... 'Well, I guess I can't do cabdrivers.'
jokes.setup: Elaine recounts the hairdresser's final insult
jokes.punchline: The devastating classist dismissal that reduces her to her job
jokes.explanation: Powerful callback that shows the lasting impact of Vincenzo's cruelty

jokes.text: every time you hear the laughter of kids... every time a policeman's horse shies up
jokes.setup: Louie listing why Elaine can't forget the hair disaster
jokes.punchline: Escalating from reasonable (kids laughing) to absurd (police horses scared of her hair)
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from plausible to ridiculous. The police horse detail is perfectly absurd and visual.

jokes.text: You come here often? I'm just here waiting for a cancellation.
jokes.setup: Alex making small talk in the fancy salon
jokes.punchline: Using pickup line in inappropriate setting, getting matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Decent fish-out-of-water humor with Alex trying to fit into upscale salon environment

jokes.text: Oh, my God! The fleet's in.
jokes.setup: Alex introducing himself as working with Elaine
jokes.punchline: Snobbish customer's reaction to more 'working class' people arriving
jokes.explanation: Good class-based humor showing salon snobbery toward working people

jokes.text: Beautiful! Like a parrot.
jokes.setup: Vincenzo's staff fake-praising Elaine's hair as 'beautiful'
jokes.punchline: Louie's deadpan agreement followed by unflattering comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie callback - agrees with the fake praise then undercuts it with bird comparison, tying back to earlier parrot joke

jokes.text: she brought the Jewish Defense League to get her money back
jokes.setup: Vincenzo seeing Alex and Louie with Elaine
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously exaggerating their presence as militant organization
jokes.explanation: Good character line showing Vincenzo's melodramatic persecution complex

jokes.text: Is this the big date you were getting ready for?
jokes.setup: Louie sees Elaine's hair disaster in the context of her romantic evening
jokes.punchline: The visual irony of her 'perfect' preparation resulting in complete disaster
jokes.explanation: Painful reminder of how the hair disaster ruins Elaine's romantic plans

jokes.text: Stay! Stay! Stay!
jokes.setup: Alex trying to control Louie like a dog
jokes.punchline: Treating Louie literally like a pet with commands
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy bit treating human like a dog, fits the desperate situation

jokes.text: Joan of Arc did less whining at the stake.
jokes.setup: Elaine's emotional speech about her hair disaster
jokes.punchline: Comparing her legitimate complaint to martyrdom whining
jokes.explanation: Brilliant villain line - historically clever, completely inappropriate, perfectly delivered. Shows Vincenzo's cruel wit at its peak.

jokes.text: She may be better than you, but I ain't.
jokes.setup: Elaine taking the high road and leaving with dignity
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately abandoning any pretense of being civilized
jokes.explanation: Perfect climactic Louie line - completely in character, great setup/payoff, and perfectly timed after Elaine's moral victory

jokes.text: Throbbing noises, flashing lights, monsters chasing you... I get enough of that in real life.
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on why he plays Pac-Man
jokes.punchline: Realizing his real life is exactly like the stressful video game
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending tag - brilliant observation about video games mirroring real stress, delivered with Jim's innocent wisdom. Great character moment.
